_meta.description: Add descriptions to 12 existing phase2_flag_types rows that currently have null description.
_meta.reason: Rows 1-12 in phase2_flag_types were inserted without description text. This patch populates description for each row using the definitions in the Session A v8 specification. Rows 13 and 14 already have descriptions and are not touched by this patch.

rows.description: God experiences, enacts, or is the direct subject of this inner state or action in at least one meaning sub-definition or verse
Term has an explicit causative grammatical form: Hiphil or Piel (Hebrew) or stated causative sense (Greek); causes the inner state in another
Meaning block or verses connect this inner state to a specific bodily organ or physical process as its seat or origin
Inner state manifests through visible outward physical behaviour such as trembling, weeping, prostration, or the face falling
Greek term directly names an inner faculty (kardia, pneuma, psuche, nous); faculty-naming warrants attention in Session B
Hebrew-Aramaic or Hebrew-Greek pair resolving to the same referent across language or testament boundary
Term shares a root family with one or more terms in another part of this split registry; root spans the part boundary
Fewer than 5 verse records or very low occurrence count; dataset too thin for confident Session B analysis
Verse count is less than 20 percent of stated occurrence count for terms with 20 or more occurrences; sample is not representative
Term appeared as a duplicate entry in the source data; resolved and recorded as a single term record with a quality note
No STEP word analysis data available for this term; meaning and verse records are absent; extraction deferred
Term is likely to merge with a closely related entry during Session B; warrants review before final dataset construction
